Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Feeds Act, the Fertilizers Act, the Seeds Act, the Pest Control Products Act and the Food and Drugs Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Feeds Act, the Fertilizers Act, the Seeds Act and the Pest Control Products Act to provide for provisional registration or approval of feeds, fertilizers, seeds and pest control products that are already approved by two or more trusted jurisdictions.

It also amends the Food and Drugs Act to provide that the Minister may, by order, deem that specified requirements of that Act or the regulations are met in respect of a veterinary drug on the basis of a decision of, or any information or document produced by, a foreign regulatory authority.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Facilitating Agricultural Regulatory Modernization Act.
Section 2 of the Feeds Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: trusted jurisdictionâmeans a foreign state or a subdivision of a foreign state that is designated by regulation;â(Ãtat de confiance)

Section.Text: Paragraph 3(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has, in accordance with the regulations, been approved by the Minister, or registered, on a provisional or final basis;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4:

Heading.TitleText: Provisional and Final Approval or Registration

Subsection.Text: If a feed is approved for manufacture, sale or import under the legislation of at least two trusted jurisdictions, the Minister must, in accordance with the regulations, approve or register the feed on a provisional basis within 90 days of the submission of a complete application for approval or registration of the feed, unless during that time, the feed is found not to be in compliance with this Act or the regulations.

Once an application for approval or registration of a feed has been evaluated, the Minister must, in accordance with the regulations, approve or register the feed on a final basis if the feed is found to be in compliance with this Act and the regulations.
Paragraphs 5(1)(b) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: designating, as trusted jurisdictions, foreign states or subdivisions of foreign states for the purposes of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the registration of feeds on a provisional or final basis and prescribing fees for registration on a final basis;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the approval of feeds on a provisional or final basis;

Paragraph.Label: (b.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, in relation to the Ministerâs evaluation of a feed on application for its registration or approval, the reliance that may be placed on evidence and information furnished in connection with the feedâs approval for manufacture, sale or import under the legislation of any trusted jurisdiction;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the duration and cancellation of the registration or approval of feeds on a provisional or final basis;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 5(1)(h) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: providing that feeds registered, on a provisional or final basis, under this Act and containing a pest control product as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Pest Control Products Act are, in prescribed circumstancesand subject to prescribed conditions, deemed to be registered under that Act;

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Fertilizers Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: trusted jurisdictionâmeans a foreign state or a subdivision of a foreign state that is designated by regulation;â(Ãtat de confiance)

Section.Text: Paragraph 3(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has, in accordance with the regulations, been approved by the Minister or registered, on a provisional or final basis;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4:

Heading.TitleText: Provisional and Final Approval or Registration

Subsection.Text: If a fertilizer or supplement is approved for manufacture, sale or import under the legislation of at least two trusted jurisdictions, the Minister must, in accordance with the regulations, approve or register the fertilizer or supplement on a provisional basis within 90 days of the submission of a complete application for approval or registration of the fertilizer or supplement, unless during that time, the fertilizer or supplement is found not to be in compliance with this Act or the regulations.

Once an application for approval or registration of a fertilizer or supplement has been evaluated, the Minister must, in accordance with the regulations, approve or register the fertilizer or supplement on a final basis if the fertilizer or supplement is found to be in compliance with this Act and the regulations.
Paragraphs 5(1)(b) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: designating, as trusted jurisdictions, foreign states or subdivisions of foreign states for the purposes of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the registration of fertilizers and supplements on a provisional or final basis and prescribing fees for registration on a final basis;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the approval of fertilizers and supplements on a provisional or final basis;

Paragraph.Label: (b.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, in relation to the Ministerâs evaluation of a fertilizer or supplement on application for its registration or approval, the reliance that may be placed on evidence and information furnished in connection with the fertilizer or supplementâs approval for manufacture, sale or import under the legislation of any trusted jurisdiction;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the duration and cancellation of the registration or approval of fertilizers and supplements on a provisional or final basis;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 5(1)(h) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: providing that fertilizers registered, on a provisional or final basis, under this Act and containing a pest control product as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Pest Control Products Act are, in prescribed circumstances and subject to prescribed conditions, deemed to be registered under that Act;

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Seeds Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: trusted jurisdictionâmeans a foreign state or a subdivision of a foreign state that is designated by regulation;â(Ãtat de confiance)

Section.Text: Paragraph 3(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sell or advertise for sale in Canada or import into Canada seed of a variety that is not registered, on a provisional or final basis, in the prescribed manner.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 3.2:

Subsection.Text: If a variety of seed is approved for sale under the legislation of at least two trusted jurisdictions, the Registrar must register the variety, in accordance with the regulations, on a provisional basis within 90 days of the submission of a complete application, as prescribed, for the registration of the variety unless, during that time, the Registrar determines that the prescribed requirements are not met.

Once an application for the registration of a variety has been evaluated, the Registrar must register the variety, in accordance with the regulations, on a final basis if the prescribed requirements are met.
For the purposes of this section, Registrar means the person designated by the President of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency to register varieties.
Subsection 4(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h.1):

Paragraph.Label: (h.11)
Paragraph.Text: designating, as trusted jurisdictions, foreign states or subdivisions of foreign states for the purposes of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (h.12)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, in relation to the evaluation of a variety of seed on application for its registration, the reliance that may be placed on evidence and information furnished in connection with the seed's approval for sale under the legislation of any trusted jurisdiction;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(1)(h.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the registration, on a provisional or final basis, of varieties of seeds and the amendment of the register of such varieties;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition conditions of registration in subsection 2(1) of the Pest Control Products Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any conditions specified by the Minister under paragraph 7.1(1)(a) or 8(1)(a) or subsection 8(2) or when amending the registration of a pest control product under this Act; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: trusted jurisdictionâmeans a foreign state or a subdivision of a foreign state that is designated by regulation.â(Ãtat de confiance)

Subsection.Text: Section 7 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2.1):

For the purposes of subsection (1), if a pest control product is approved for use under the legislation of at least two trusted jurisdictions, the applicant may apply to register the pest control product on a provisional basis, in accordance with the regulations, by including information that is available from the reviews or evaluations of the pest control product conducted by the relevant authorities of those trusted jurisdictions.
The portion of subsection 7(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
If the Minister is satisfied that the application has been made in accordance with any of subsections (1) to (2.2), the Minister shall
Subsection 7(9) of the Act is replaced by the following:
In determining whether the health and environmental risks and the value of a pest control product are acceptable, the Minister may, in accordance with the regulations, if any, take into account

Paragraph.Text: information regarding the risks and value of other pest control products that are registered for the same use; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information that is available from a review or evaluation of the pest control product conducted by the relevant authority of a trusted jurisdiction.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7:

Subsection.Text: If, within 90 days of the submission of a complete application made in accordance with subsections 7(1) and (2.2), the Minister does not determine that the health and environmental risks and the value of the pest control product are unacceptable, the Minister must register the pest control product on a provisional basis in accordance with the regulations, if any, by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the conditions relating to its manufacture, handling, storage, transport, import, export, packaging, distribution, use or disposition, including conditions relating to its composition, and, subject to subsection 8(2), the conditions relating to its label; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: assigning a registration number to the product.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Provision of safety information to workplaces
Subsection.Text: Without limiting the generality of paragraph (1)(a), the Minister must specify, as a condition of registration on a provisional basis, the requirement for product safety information, including a material safety data sheet for the product, to be provided to workplaces where the pest control product is used or manufactured, in accordance with the regulations made under paragraph 67(1)(s).
Registration of a pest control product on a provisional basis is valid until either the pest control product is registered under section 8 or the application for registration is withdrawn or denied.

Section.Text: Subparagraphs 28(1)(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to register under section 8 a pest control product that is or contains an unregistered active ingredient, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to register or amend the registration of, under section 8, a pest control product if the Minister considers that registration or amendment of the registration may result in significantly increased health or environmental risks;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 67(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: designating, as trusted jurisdictions, foreign states or subdivisions of foreign states for the purposes of this Act;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 67(1)(f) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the registration and provisional registration of pest control products, including the types of registration for classes of products, and, for each type,

Section.Text: Subsection 30.06(1) of the Food and Drugs Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2) and any regulations made under paragraph 30(1)(j.1), the Minister may, by order, deem that specified requirements of this Act or the regulations are met â in respect of a therapeutic product, a veterinary drug or food that belongs to a class specified in the order â on the basis of a decision of, or any information or document produced by, a foreign regulatory authority in respect of that therapeutic product, veterinary drug or food.
